title: Longer words and patterns
description: Build endurance with 5-7 letter words — the bulk of everyday writing
text: before today we shared every story under the wide blue sky and watched the river flow

tips.text: The longer words are where everyday writing actually lives. At this pace they start to feel ordinary, which is exactly what you want.
A long word is not one frightening thing; it is a few familiar pieces in a row. Break it at the syllable and type the pieces.

tips.target: before today we shared every story under the

tips.text: Keep the wrists level, not bent up or dropped. Good posture is what keeps you typing clean past the first tired minute.

tips.target: wide blue sky and watched the river flow

character_tips.anna: A long word is only short patterns strung together. You already know the patterns; trust your hands to find them. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: Setting a long word down without a single slip is real ground gained. Stamina counts as much as speed on a long page. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: Five and six letters at a stretch. Hold the rhythm and the long ones go by no slower than the short. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: Bigger words, same steady hand. You are building the endurance that real writing asks for. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
